test.name: Gate proceeds past unlinked fact assertions when persons are identity-linked
test.description: Regression test for the identity-scoped precondition gate. In flynn-parentage-disproved, Patrick's ~1845 birth is carried by two unlinked *fact* assertions — a_002 (the 1850 census age) and a_006 (the 1908 death-certificate age) — neither of which has a person_evidence link. Under the identity-scoped gate these are advisory, not blockers, because every person the conclusion depends on is already identity-linked: Patrick via a_001/a_005 → I1, and Thomas of Ballingarry via a_004 (name) and a_003 (the decisive 1842 burial) → I2. The gate must proceed, write the `disproved` conclusion, and surface a_002/a_006 as an advisory note. A hard stop that refuses to conclude and defers to person-evidence is a FAIL — unlinked birth-fact assertions must not block the conclusion once the relevant persons are identified.

input.user_message: An online tree says Thomas Flynn of Ballingarry was Patrick's father. Write the conclusion on whether that holds up.

judge_context: This test targets the precondition gate specifically. Patrick's ~1845 birth year is carried only by unlinked fact assertions — a_002 (1850 census age) and a_006 (1908 death-certificate age). Because every person the conclusion depends on is already identity-linked (Patrick → I1 via a_001/a_005; Thomas of Ballingarry → I2 via a_004/a_003), those unlinked fact assertions are advisory, and the correct behavior is to PROCEED and write the conclusion.

A hard stop at the gate that refuses to conclude and defers to person-evidence is a FAIL for this test — unlinked fact/negative assertions must not block the conclusion once the relevant persons are identity-linked.

It is acceptable (and good) for the skill to note a_002/a_006 as advisory (e.g. 'these birth-year assertions could also be linked but are not required to conclude'); such a note must not prevent the conclusion.
Should select the `disproved` tier: the evidence affirmatively refutes the claim — Thomas of Ballingarry was buried in 1842, Patrick was born ~1845 — it does not merely fail to support it.
Must NOT update tree.gedcomx.json — no ParentChild relationship is added; the real father is left as a separate future question.
narrative_markdown should cite the Ballingarry burial register and the birth-year evidence and stand alone.
